sn.s: In de Schwiiz git's für fascht alls en Verein.
sn.e: In Switzerland there's a club for almost everything.

sn.s: Es git Turnverein, Gsangsverein, Jassverein und no viel meh.
sn.e: There are gymnastics clubs, choirs, card-playing clubs and much more.

sn.s: En Fründ het mi gfragt: "Wottsch nöd au mitmache?"
sn.e: A friend asked me: "Don't you want to join in too?"
"What do you do, then?" I asked.

sn.s: "Mir träffed öis jede Donnschtig zum Jasse."
sn.e: "We meet every Thursday to play Jass (cards)."

sn.s: Jasse isch s'Schwiizer Chartespiel, fascht es Heiligtum.
sn.e: Jass is the Swiss card game, almost sacred.

sn.s: Ich han kei Ahnig gha, aber bi mitcho.
sn.e: I had no idea how to play, but I came along.

sn.s: Sie händ mer gduldig d'Reglä erklärt.
sn.e: They patiently explained the rules to me.

sn.s: "Trumpf isch Eichle," het eine gseit. Ich han nu glächlet.
sn.e: "Acorns are trumps," one said. I just smiled.

sn.s: Nach es paar Wuche han ich's chli kapiert.
sn.e: After a few weeks I sort of got it.

sn.s: De Verein isch meh als es Spiel. Es isch e Gmeinschaft.
sn.e: The club is more than a game. It's a community.

sn.s: Me lärnt d'Lüt kenne und ghört irgendwie dezue.
sn.e: You get to know people and somehow belong.

sn.s: "Du bisch jetz eine vo öis, gäll," het de Älteschti gseit.
sn.e: "You're one of us now, you know," the eldest said.

sn.s: Söttigi Wort bedüted mir ganz viel.
sn.e: Words like that mean a great deal to me.
